Hi there, my name is Mark McCray and I've teamed up with Bond and Kevin Harbert to bring you Habitology. I joined forces with Bond and Kevin because of my great respect for the late father Gary Harbert. For those of you who have never heard of him, he was in my opinion, and many other people's opinion, one of the greatest copywriters and marketers of all time. Over the years I've made millions of dollars offline and online in countries all over the world, countries like America, South Africa, Canada, Australia. I've built and bought multiple businesses and helped save a few from ruin. It wasn't always easy, which is one of the reasons I think I associate so closely with Gary. He never gave up. Not many people know that it took him a year and a half to write his first successful sales letter. How many of you would persevere for over a year on something you were unsuccessful at? Gary was that type of person. He was also unique in that he shared his knowledge and philosophy of life. There have of course been other great marketers and copywriters, but very few were also great teachers and also prepared to share their knowledge willingly. So in the spirit of Gary Harbert, I would like to share with you some of the things I have learned along the way of building multiple businesses. I find it interesting that both successful businesses and people just starting suffer from the same thing. For successful businesses, one of the major causes for their failure or for them to stop growing is their success. I know it sounds strange. As Greg McCown describes in his book, Essentialism, success means more opportunities and the more opportunities you have, the less likely you are to succeed. The cause of your failure is success. When you say yes to too many things, you invariably lose focus. There is no general who wants to fight on multiple fronts. The same is true when you are just starting out. Only this time, it is not because of success that gives you too many choices. It is because you are constantly seeking out the next best thing or the next best hack or to try and solve your problems. This abundance of choice and inability to focus affects both groups equally. In a minute, I am going to share with you some of the things that will help you focus and grow your business regardless of what size you are. But first, I want to share with you an observation that I have learned the hard way. I believe that business is a holistic activity. It is not about one thing. It is not about the best strategy, the best copy, the best process, or the best any one thing. It is all of them combined. It is about a lot of different things working together effectively and efficiently to produce a result. I think one reason so many entrepreneurs struggle in the beginning is that they are convinced they are missing a piece of the puzzle. Every successful marketer I know is also a product junkie. It is almost like we cannot help ourselves. And it is also one of the reasons you can fail. Fail to start. It is fine to buy the next course or service, but almost no one executes on the information because by the time the information arrives or they have time to look at it, they are already on to the next shiny object. It is this never-ending eternal search for answers. It is kind of like the people who have been telling me for years I need a balanced life. For years people have been trying to tell me that the perfect life is when you have things that are important to you in balance. Well, I believe life is not balanced. It cannot be pigeonholed into perfect life-size bits. In fact, the more successful you are, the more unbalanced you will probably be in the areas that you are successful. And that is a good thing. That is why I think harmony is a lot more important. If your life is in harmony with you and the people around you, then you will probably feel a lot better about yourself and you almost cannot help but become more successful in the areas of your life and all areas of your life. It is the same for business. You don't have to have every part nailed down to be successful. Most businesses have one thing or one or two things they are good at. They can lose focus by trying to be good at all things, but you can't be good at all things. It is better to focus on a few things that you are good at and outsource the rest. It is a mistake to focus on the things you are not good at. In my own journey to success and the experience of owning over 30 businesses all over the world and employing thousands of people, one truth shines through. As an entrepreneur, your business is a reflection of you and who you are. The culture of your business, especially when you are a small business, is basically your personality. If you are the type of person who likes to be in control and is very process driven, then your business will reflect that. The people who work for you will feel it too. They know when they are working for a passionate driven person who has a vision of where they want to go and who they want to take that. They also know when they are working for a person who is self-centred, egotistical and believes that it is all about them. You get my point. As the owner of a small business, a lot of what happens and how your business is perceived comes down to you. I am going to switch over to my computer now so I can give you an overview of some of the things that can help grow your business. So let's have a look at some of these things. First, business doesn't need to be hard. There are basically two parts to the equation. You need a product or service to sell and an audience or potential buyers. Bond and Kevin along with some of the brightest minds in marketing today will be covering which markets are good and how to talk to and convert prospects into buyers. My role in halberdology is to share with people my experience of running and building businesses from scratch into successful and profitable businesses. It doesn't need to be hard. Most of the work has been done for you. For example, outsourcing. Many people consider me to be one of the leading experts in outsourcing mainly because of the results I have helped businesses achieve. I have probably hired over 1000 outsourcers from different companies. This is an example of feedback from a company where I have hired over 200 people at an average rate of $6.06 for a total of 51,091 hours. That's $309,611 I have paid to this company alone. Even if you are a one man show, you should be thinking of hiring someone to help so you can leverage your time better. Understanding and being able to outsource correctly is one of the fastest ways to grow your business and make more money. It's simple maths. If you can employ someone at $5 per hour and they produce $10 per hour, how many people would you hire? The problem is that most businesses think their business is unique or that there are certain tasks that are too complicated for an outsourcer. This is to your advantage because you will be able to dominate any market once you learn how to apply the power of outsourcing. Many people are surprised to find that I do a lot of my outsourcing in America, Canada and UK. I have a well thought out tried and tested recruitment process that reduces the time you spend hiring and the chances of hiring the wrong person. I have basically reduced this to a process where I can onboard people in any amount for a task or job. By the time they have finished the onboarding process, it's like they have been working with me for years. It's a lot simpler than it might sound. I have everything mapped out and ready to be shared. Anyone could run my business. I have strived to have every part of my business reduced to a process. I have the equivalent of a McDonald's owner manual where every part of my business is documented and printed out. An average business man could probably successfully take over my business in a few weeks. That's exactly how I want it. It's the reason I can take months off at a time and work only a few hours a week when I chose to. It doesn't matter what size your business is, you should be looking at certain key numbers such as daily leads and daily sales. You should also be thinking in terms of process even if you are one man's show because you can't effectively bring on more people and measure your business without it. For example, in my business, each day we review our customer care team calls to see how many we made, how many we reached, and how many we actually spoke with. We monitor our refunds on a daily basis to see how they are performing in relation to our historical records. We also monitor how many live chats, emails, comments posted, and Skype chats we have. One of the other benefits of building a process and measuring your results is that it allows you to be independent of thought. It doesn't matter what anyone tells you, your results will give you all the answers you need to know if you act on them. Tracking vital stats in building processes is simple if you follow some basic guidelines and use some simple software. Most of the time it is just basic common sense in a checklist. I will be sharing the simple tools I use to do these activities with the halvotology members. Now let's talk about traffic. Every niche is different and we find that certain traffic sources work better on some markets than others. Joint venture traffic is good but we have been focusing more and more on media buys. Here are a few pointers. If you can't measure the traffic, don't buy or invest money and time into it. Figure out what type of traffic is the best converting for your niche. 50 to 80% of your time should be spent on figuring out how to get more traffic. People are also surprised when I tell them that I almost never use pay per click or SEO. I have done in the past but I have found better, easier ways to buy qualified traffic. My traffic building process is so simple almost anyone could learn it fast. Certainly a lot faster than setting up a PPC campaign or watching hours of video. At some stage if you want to grow big you need to be able to find and convert cold traffic. You will eventually capture or exhaust your affiliate base or warm leads and need to look outside of those pools. If you get the traffic part right it makes everything else easier. In this example I got 2392 leads at 36 cents per lead. That's in a niche where I normally pay $5 per lead. I'm not saying you will get dirt cheap traffic from the first day you implement my traffic template but I do think that more people would have success with this than most of the traditional ways they are getting traffic. The last thing I want to cover in this video is email marketing. We have developed a way to build autoresponder sequences better than anything I have seen or bought. It's not unusual for us to get over 7% conversion on the very first email or over 18% on upsells. If you combine the right traffic with the right autoresponder sequence and the right sales funnel you have built your own little profitable business. We constantly tweak our copy until we get to the conversion rate we need to be profitable. The sales funnel on the left made $144,404 on autopilots so far once we found the right combination of traffic and copy. How many assets that make $144,000 on autopilot do you want? Think of the sales funnel as an asset. Once it's set up and in place it requires very little work. So my question to you is if you knew you could build a sales funnel that makes $100,000 a month how many sales funnels would you make? The majority of the money you will make if you do this right will be in the follow up sequence. This is where most marketers fail. They don't know how often to email, what to say and how to keep the conversation going. It's in the follow up sequence where you will find your part of gold. Remember the image of the autoresponder with the sequence of emails? Well we carefully monitor every email, its open rate, its click through rate and how many sales the email makes. What do you think we do if we see that an email is not converting the way we would like? We pull that email from the sequence and test another one. We keep doing this until we get the results we want. That's how you make a lot of money with email marketing. Let's look at conversion. Conversion is more about how qualified the audience you are talking to is than any other single thing. The four things that affect conversion the most and in order of importance are you need to be speaking to a starving crowd, you need to be speaking their language, you need a great offer and you need great copy. If you nail these four things you will make a lot of money. If I had to choose just one thing it would be the starving crowd. Not only do we test the copy for example, we test the font, size of the text, should it have an indent at the start of the paragraph, should you use high or hey or have no name in the subject line, how many links are the most effective, should you use long or short copy, should it have a PS or no PS, what parts of the text should be bold. We use a comprehensive checklist to improve any email we are testing. If you take average copy and systematically test it you can double, triple and even quadruple your results with small improvements. It might never be great copy but you have to ask yourself if the objective is to have great copy or great sales. Testing will make you rich. Here's a question, if you have a gun with one bullet in it and your life depends on hitting a target how confident would you be that you could hit the target at 100 yards? Probably not very confident. Now imagine you have a machine gun and you have unlimited bullets, how confident are you that you could hit a target at 100 yards? Probably very confident right? Well testing is like that, you have virtually unlimited chances to improve your copy. I have yet to see anyone exhaust the possibilities of how to improve on their conversion rate. It reminds me of a movie that came out years ago called Groundhog Day, starring Bill Murray. Each day he woke up it was the exact same day. He got to relive the same day until he made it perfect. Testing is like that, you can test until you can make it perfect. I am passionate about business and I love to work with the right people. People with the passion and drive for growing their business and then scaling them up to the point where they are able to achieve all of their financial and personal goals and even become wealthy in the process. It really is easier than you think when you have all the right pieces of the puzzle. I hope you have a chance to meet in the members area where I want to bring you even more actionable proven strategies and ideas for you to implement that you can use to grow your business. But for now, thanks for taking the time to watch my video.
